page: Microsoft Azure FX96-24ms_v2 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the FXmsv2 family, virtualized via Microsoft Hyper-V. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz, configured with 12 physical cores and 24 vCPUs. The processor includes a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and AMX. The system is equipped with 1832.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 76.33 GB per core. No local storage is bundled with this instance, though it is premium storage capable, and it does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates strong memory subsystem performance and single-core compute capabilities. Large-block uncached memory read bandwidth reaches 187.06 GB/sec, and write bandwidth is recorded at 118.92 GB/sec, both placing in the top 25% tier. Single-core performance is notable, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2366.0 and a text processing score of 2974.0, both ranking in the top 10% tier. Multi-core performance is also robust, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 16770.0 and single-threaded gzip compression throughput of 34.65 GB/sec, both falling in the top 25%. Other operations, such as Redis SET operations at 38.78 million operations per second and static web serving throughput at 36.14 GB/sec, perform in the middle 50% tier.

From a resource density perspective, the FX96-24ms_v2 offers high qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads due to its massive memory allocation relative to its 24 vCPUs. However, the lack of local storage represents a key tradeoff, requiring external premium storage attachments for data persistence. The server is highly suited for memory-intensive applications, large-scale database caching, in-memory analytics, and workloads requiring high single-core CPU frequencies alongside substantial RAM capacity.
description: Microsoft Azure FX96-24ms_v2 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server from the FXmsv2 family, powered by a dedicated 12-core Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz with 24 vCPUs. It features an exceptionally high memory capacity of 1832.0 GB (76.33 GB per core) and a 260 MB L3 cache, but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. In benchmarks, the server delivers top-tier single-core performance, with Geekbench single-core and text processing scores in the top 10%, alongside strong large-block memory bandwidth in the top 25%. While its lack of local storage requires external storage configurations, its massive memory density and high clock speed make it highly efficient for memory-intensive workloads, in-memory databases, and heavy single-threaded processing tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure FX96-24ms_v2 is a memory-optimized server featuring a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor with 24 vCPUs, 1832 GB of RAM, and top-tier single-core benchmark performance.

tagline: A memory-intensive server combining a high-frequency 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor with an expansive 1.8 TB memory allocation.

Massive 1832.0 GB of system memory providing 76.33 GB per core
Top-tier single-core and text processing performance in the top 10%
